chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 382 of 2020 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case (priority) 1. For orders on office objection at Flag-A 2. For orders on CMA No.3078/2020 (151 CPC) 3. For hearing of CMA No.2664/2020 (C/A) 4. For hearing of CAM No.1917/2020 (S/A) 5. For hearing of main case 01-10-2020 Syed Muhammad Ali Shah Rizvi, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Chandio, Additional Advocate General Sindh and Mr. Shahryar Imadad Awan, Assistant Advocate General Sindh along with Adnan Rashid, Additional Deputy Commissioner-I, Sukkur and Zahid Ali Shah, Mukhtiarkar, Rohri..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Comments have been filed by Respondents 2 to 4 and 6, which are taken on record. Copy of the same is provided to learned counsel for the Petitioner. 2. Learned counsel for the Petitioner submits that previously in the year 2004-05, the District Government, Sukkur reserved the land in Deh Nandho Kohistan, Taluka Rohri for establishing the Cattle Colony, Rohri, and they have spent Rs.20 million on the said project. 3. In response to that, the Additional Deputy Commissioner-I, Sukkur, who is appearing on behalf of the Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur, submits that though the establishment of Cattle Colony, Rohri was approved by the Defunct District Government, Sukkur at an estimated cost of Rs.20 million in the year 2004-05, but no such record relating to that scheme is available in the office of Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur (Respondent No.2), as that District Government was dissolved in the year 2011. He further submits that the Executive Engineer, Buildings Division, Sukkur, who was the executing agency of that scheme, has briefly mentioned that

In response to that, the Additional Deputy Commissioner-I, Sukkur, who is appearing on behalf of the Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur, submits that though the establishment of Cattle Colony, Rohri was approved by the Defunct District Government, Sukkur at an estimated cost of Rs.20 million in the year 2004-05, but no such record relating to that scheme is available in the office of Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur (Respondent No.2), as that District Government was dissolved in the year 2011. He further submits that the Executive Engineer, Buildings Division, Sukkur, who was the executing agency of that scheme, has briefly mentioned that C. P. No. D – 382 of 2020 Page 2 of 2 cost of Rs.10.382 million was released for the said scheme from the year 2004-05 to 2008-09. He further points out that major portion of the said earmarked land, which was previously proposed for establishment of Cattle Colony, Rohri situated in Deh Nandho Kohistan, has been acquired by the National Highway Authority, as Motorway (Multan-Sukkur) Section has crossed from the said land. He, however, submits another proposal for Cattle Colony, Rohri before this Court today, in which it is mentioned that an area of about 13-00 acres situated in Deh Dandho Kohistan out of previously proposed Cattle Colony, Rohri in the year 2004 is lying vacant, and the same can be utilized for the subject purpose. 4. The Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur is directed to collect the applications from the owners of cattle for the purpose of shifting the cattle ponds and allot the land to each and every owner of cattle for the subject purpose after completing all codal formalities. This exercise should be completed within two months, and such compliance report be submitted to this Court through the Additional Registrar of this Court. Copy of this order be sent to the Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur for compliance. This petition along with listed applications is disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
